description.summary: Add --from-git flag to /tc retro that auto-analyzes git log and generates retro_changelog.json without requiring manual JSON authoring
description.motivation: The current /tc retro requires users to manually write a retro_changelog.json file, which is tedious for projects with extensive history. Auto-generating from git log removes this friction and makes onboarding existing projects much faster. Originally specified in GitHub issue #1 but the --from-git mode was not implemented.
description.detailed_design: Python script that runs git log, parses commits, groups related commits into logical TCs by file overlap and timeframe, extracts metadata (files changed, dates, tags), and outputs a valid retro_changelog.json. Can be used standalone or piped into the existing retro generator.

files_affected.description: Git log parser with commit clustering, scope auto-detection, retro_changelog.json output

revision_history.author: ai:claude-opus-4-6

handoff.key_context: Must use Python stdlib only to match existing generator conventions. Git log parsing should handle merge commits and squash merges gracefully.
